connections.description: According to Herodotus, Barca came to be founded ca. 560 BCE following a quarrel between King Arcesilaus II of Cyrene and his brothers. The brothers quit Cyrene and founded Barca.

names.details: Berḳa: It is a town in the easternmost part of the West [the Maghreb]. However, there are existing ruins showing that it was a large city in the past. It is situated by [or dependent on] a river named Dernâ. During the caliphate of Al-Mutawakkil, a city wall was built around it, and because it is an extremely stony place, it was called Berḳa. However, its stones are of various colors. In short, it is located between Egypt and Ifriqiya, and it is widely known that Egypt's tar and wine mostly come from there. [translation]
